hero.title: Dawah Norway
hero.quran: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction, and argue with them in a way that is best.

home.donateText: Kindly donate generously and contribute to our ongoing dawah efforts across Norway
home.newMuslimText: Whether you're new to Islam or still learning the basics, our New Muslims section is here to support you with clear, encouraging resources every step of the way.
home.whoWeAreTitle: Who We Are and What We Do
home.whoWeAreText: Dawah Norway is a non-profit organization founded in 2021, dedicated to educating both Muslim and non-Muslim communities about Islam through information tables, dawah stands, and organized classes. We operate across six cities in Norway with local teams in Oslo, Østfold, Kristiansand, Stavanger, Trondheim, and Tromsø.
home.whyOutreachText: Creating connections and fostering understanding between Muslim and non-Muslim communities across Norway
home.whyQuranText: Our cornerstone effort providing authentic Quranic access to everyone who seeks knowledge
home.whyWorkshopText: A welcoming platform for individuals embracing Islam, providing guidance and community support
home.exploreWhyText: Discover the timeless message of Islam — submission to One God, inner peace, and a clear purpose in life.
home.exploreMuhammadText: The final Messenger of God. Explore his life, character, and the mercy he brought to humanity.
home.exploreNewText: Essential guides, courses, and resources to support your journey — whether you have embraced Islam or are still exploring.
home.glimpsesText: Moments captured from dawah stands, Quran distributions, and community gatherings across Norway.

newMuslims.intro: Whether you have recently embraced Islam or are exploring the faith, this page provides essential resources to guide you on your journey.
newMuslims.becomeMuslimText: Take the first step on your journey of faith

newMuslims.purposeText: Understanding the meaning and purpose of life in Islam
newMuslims.ghusalText: Learn how to perform the purification shower
newMuslims.prayerText: Learn the Islamic prayer step by step

about.title: About Us
about.bismillah: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
about.intro: Dawah Norway is a non-profit organization founded in 2021 in Norway, dedicated to educating both Muslim and non-Muslim communities about Islam through information tables, dawah stands, and organized classes.
about.historyText: Founded in 2021, Dawah Norway has grown into an active community inviting people to Islam with wisdom and compassion. We adhere to the Qur'an and authentic Sunnah, following the 'Aqidah of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah. With six local teams across Norway — in Oslo, Østfold, Kristiansand, Stavanger, Trondheim, and Tromsø — we actively work to share the message of Islam.
about.quranVerse: Let there be a group among you who call others to goodness, encourage what is good, and forbid what is evil — it is they who will be successful.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.statement: Our mission is to educate both Muslim and non-Muslim communities about Islam through outreach, distribution of authentic Islamic resources, and organized community programs across Norway.
mission.educationText: A welcoming platform for individuals embracing Islam, providing comprehensive guidance and community support
mission.outreachText: Creating connections and fostering understanding through dawah stands, information tables, and organized classes across six cities
mission.counselingText: Inspiring and nurturing the next generation of Muslims in Norway through mentorship and educational programs

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.statement: We envision a Norway where every individual has access to authentic Islamic knowledge, where communities are united by shared understanding and respect for one another.
vision.intro: Dawah Norway is committed to inviting people to Islam and bridging cultural and belief-based gaps through educational initiatives across Norway.
vision.impactText: Over 1,000 people have accepted Islam through our efforts. We have distributed over 50,000 free Quran copies and 80,000+ Islamic literature items across Norway.
vision.priority1: Expanding dawah operations to every major city in Norway
vision.priority2: Establishing collaborative partnerships with Islamic organizations across Norway and beyond
vision.priority3: Developing sustainable programs for new Muslim support and youth empowerment
vision.cta: Join us in making a difference through volunteering, donations, and spreading the word of Islam.

whyIslam.title: Why Islam?
whyIslam.intro: Islam is the universal message of submission to the One God. Here are key reasons why millions embrace this timeless faith.
whyIslam.s1Text: Islam emphasizes a personal bond with God. This connection encourages believers to maintain constant awareness of the divine, forming the foundation of lasting happiness. The faith teaches that peace originates from God, and through nurturing this relationship and following divine guidance, believers achieve inner peace.
whyIslam.s1Quote: Truly, in remembering God do hearts find rest.
whyIslam.s2Text: Islam provides perspective by viewing life's events as divine tests. Believers are encouraged to interpret occurrences within life's broader purpose: acknowledging and obeying God. The faith advocates focusing on controllable aspects, expressing gratitude for blessings, and practicing patience during hardship — creating contentment and preventing life's extremes.
whyIslam.s3Title: Pure and Clear Concept of God
whyIslam.s3Text: Islam emphasizes God's absolute perfection and uniqueness. God is singular, without partners or equals, and solely worthy of worship. The faith stresses divine power, perfection, and supreme nature across all realms.
whyIslam.s4Text: Islam encourages using intellect to contemplate existence and the cosmos. The Quran serves as proof of divine origin through its preservation, universal message, linguistic beauty, and scientific foreknowledge.
whyIslam.s5Text: Islam balances hope in divine mercy with fear of judgement. Humans possess autonomy to err but can seek forgiveness through sincere repentance, which strengthens their bond with God without requiring intermediaries.
whyIslam.s6Text: Every individual faces accountability for their actions. The Day of Judgement ensures equitable retribution and divine justice, nurturing harmonious societies grounded in fairness.
whyIslam.s7Title: Practical and Balanced Way of Life
whyIslam.s7Text: Islam harmonizes belief with action, addressing spiritual, physical, psychological, and societal needs. Daily prayers and annual fasting provide purpose, humility, and community connection.
whyIslam.s8Text: Islam's message applies to all humanity across ages, inviting personal spiritual journeys based on faith and righteousness rather than earthly distinctions.
whyIslam.ctaTitle: Ready to take the next step?
whyIslam.ctaText: Explore how to embrace Islam and connect with our community.

muhammad.title: Who is Muhammad ﷺ?
muhammad.intro: Muslims regard Muhammad (peace be upon him) as the final Prophet in a succession that includes Adam, Noah, Abraham, Moses, David, Solomon, and Jesus. His life is the living embodiment of the Qur'an.
muhammad.aishaQuote: His character was a reflection of the Quran.
muhammad.missionText: Muhammad ﷺ emphasized that authentic faith manifests through righteous conduct and compassionate treatment. He taught that the best of people are those with the best character, and stressed mercy toward all.
muhammad.missionQuote: And We have not sent you (Muhammad) except as a mercy for mankind.
muhammad.forgivenessText: Muhammad ﷺ exemplified extreme patience and forgiveness, particularly when possessing power to retaliate.
muhammad.forgivenessQuote: Keep to forgiveness (O Muhammad), and enjoin kindness, and turn away from the ignorant.
muhammad.equalityText: He taught universal human equality — that no race, colour or status raises one above another; only piety does.
muhammad.equalityQuote: All humanity is from Adam, and Adam is from clay. There is no superiority for an Arab over a non-Arab, nor for a black over a white, except through piety.
muhammad.toleranceText: Muhammad ﷺ taught self-control in the face of provocation.
muhammad.toleranceQuote: The strong is not the one who overcomes people by his strength, but the strong is the one who controls himself while in anger.
muhammad.gentlenessText: A ten-year companion noted his consistent gentleness. He advised his wife that gentleness beautifies everything it touches.
muhammad.humblenessText: He refused special treatment, sitting wherever space permitted and mixing with the poor. He cautioned against excessive praise, reminding his followers that he was only the Lord's servant.
muhammad.husbandText: He encouraged companions to emulate his example in family life, saying the most perfect believers are the best in morals — and the best among them are those who are best to their wives.
muhammad.gandhiQuote: It was the rigid simplicity, the utter self-effacement of the Prophet, the scrupulous regard for his pledges, his intense devotion to his friends and followers, his intrepidity, his fearlessness, his absolute trust in God and in his own mission. These and not the sword carried everything before them and surmounted every obstacle.
muhammad.shawQuote: The world is in dire need of a man with the mind of Muhammad. I came to the conclusion that he was never an enemy of Christianity, and must be called instead the saviour of humanity.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.intro: Moments from our dawah stands, community events, lectures, and Quran distributions across Norway.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Dawah Norway is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and the rights you have.
privacy.collectText: We collect information you voluntarily share with us through our contact form (name, email, country, message, and optional phone number) and basic technical data (IP address, browser type) for security purposes.
privacy.useText: We use your information only to respond to your inquiries, support your journey with Islamic resources, and improve the quality of our outreach. We never sell or share your data with third parties for marketing.
privacy.cookiesText: We use essential cookies required for the site to function and optional analytics cookies that help us understand how the site is used. You can control cookie preferences at any time.
privacy.rightsText: You have the right to access, correct, or delete the personal information we hold about you. To exercise these rights, contact us at info@dawahnorway.com.
privacy.contactText: For any questions about this privacy policy, reach out to info@dawahnorway.com or call +47 489 20 006.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using this website, you agree to the following terms. Please read them carefully.
terms.useText: This website is provided for educational and informational purposes about Islam. You agree to use it lawfully and respectfully.
terms.donationsText: All donations are voluntary and non-refundable unless required by applicable law. Donations are processed through third-party providers (Vipps, PayPal, Stripe) and subject to their respective terms.
terms.contentText: The content on this site is provided in good faith. We do our best to ensure accuracy but make no warranty of completeness or suitability for any specific purpose.
terms.linksText: We link to third-party websites for reference. We are not responsible for the content or practices of external sites.
terms.changesText: We may update these terms periodically. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.
terms.contactText: Questions about these terms? Email info@dawahnorway.com.
